subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV, EC, Eurostat, OECD, WJP, RSF, TI, V-Dem) + Slovak independent press (Dennik N, SME) read indirectly via Anglophone aggregators (Balkan Insight, IPI, RFE/RL).
A EUROZONE/NATO member experiencing institutional backsliding under a populist, Russia-friendly government.

Standout drags: rule-of-law and press backsliding, a polarised Russia-friendly populist government, geopolitical unreliability, and demographic decline; a sound eurozone economy and low crime offset. Source-lean: MODERATE Anglophone.

economic.notes: A sound eurozone economy and an auto-export base, against an elevated deficit and EV-transition concentration risk.

fiscal_state.notes: An elevated deficit (~4-4.6%) with debt rising past ~64%, consolidation underway but with a structural gap. (IMF Art IV 2026; EC)

monetary_independence.notes: Pooled at the ECB - credible but not Slovak-controlled. (ECB)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The euro confers a modest reserve-status benefit. (IMF COFER)

inflation.notes: Eased near target, with a consolidation/transaction-tax bump a watch-item. (ECB; NBS)

banking_stability.notes: Sound, well-capitalised, and foreign-owned, with bank-levy/transaction-tax pressure. (NBS FSR; S&P)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: An auto-export powerhouse deeply German-supply-chain-integrated - real capacity but a concentration and EV-transition single-point-of-failure. (GLOBSEC; EC)

institutional.notes: Credible statistics, against active rule-of-law and press backsliding.

rule_of_law.notes: Active backsliding - the special anti-corruption prosecutor abolished, a fast-tracked penal-code cut to corruption penalties, and court pressure. (EC Rule of Law; V-Dem; TI)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Functional, with politicisation pressure and EU-funds-freeze risk. (OECD; EC)

press_freedom.notes: Declining (RSF fell sharply) - a public-broadcaster takeover and hostility to media, atop the 2018 Kuciak-murder legacy. (RSF 2026; IPI)

political_social.notes: A polarised Russia-friendly populist government, against strong baseline liberties and demographic decline.

political_stability.notes: A populist, Russia-friendly government, deeply polarised, with a 2024 assassination attempt on the prime minister and mass protests - democratic but high-friction. (RFE/RL; Dennik N/SME indirect)

civil_liberties.notes: Pressured - a 2025 NGO-transparency law and open LGBT hostility - though courts still push back. (Balkan Insight; Freedom House)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: EU/lawful, with Ukrainian-refugee support periodically threatened in energy spats. (UNHCR)

social_cohesion.notes: Deeply polarised on an urban-rural cleavage, with Russia/Ukraine as a wedge. (V-Dem; OSW)

demographics.notes: Low fertility (~1.49), rapid ageing, and rural emigration. (OSW; Eurostat)

geopolitical.notes: A NATO/EU member but a Russia-friendly spoiler, with no direct conflict.

alliance_reliability.notes: A NATO/EU member but a Russia-friendly spoiler - Ukraine-aid skepticism and threats to block sanctions/accession. (RFE/RL)

conflict_involvement.notes: No direct war, Ukraine-adjacent with no frontline. (observed)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: An EU regime with an open capital account despite obstruction politics. (EU)

physical_practical.notes: Good EU-funded infrastructure and low crime, against moderate flood/heat exposure.

climate.notes: Temperate Central-European with good readiness, and Carpathian/Danube flood and drought exposure. (ND-GAIN; EEA)

healthcare.notes: Universal but strained, with an ageing cost surge and medic emigration. (OECD)

infrastructure.notes: Good and EU-funded, with cohesion-freeze risk a caveat. (EC)

disaster_insurance.notes: A flood market with moderate penetration. (observed)

summary: A eurozone and NATO member with a sound, foreign-owned banking sector, an auto-export economy deeply tied to German supply chains, and low crime - decisively offset by institutional backsliding. Under a populist, Russia-friendly government the special anti-corruption prosecutor was abolished, corruption penalties were cut, the public broadcaster was taken over, and press freedom has fallen sharply; the government acts as an EU spoiler on Ukraine aid and sanctions. Deep polarisation (with a 2024 assassination attempt on the prime minister), an elevated deficit, an EV-transition concentration risk, and demographic decline complete a negative profile.
